organization_or_team: School or learning program support team
review_owner: Program director or student support lead
trust_context: The team wants AI to reduce routine student-support load without turning a struggling student into a ticket, a risk score, or an automated discipline path.
stakeholders: Parent, guardian, or trusted adult when appropriate

surface_steps.step: Collect routine context about the support request
surface_steps.reason: AI can collect basic scheduling, course, assignment, and access details when the student has a visible path to human help.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: A long or confusing intake form may make the student feel ignored before any adult has listened.

surface_steps.step: Prepare a private support summary for the teacher or coach
surface_steps.reason: AI can organize dates, missing work, access issues, and prior attempts so the human can respond faster.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: The summary may overstate certainty, flatten context, or miss signs that the student needs a human conversation.

surface_steps.step: Respond to discouragement, embarrassment, anxiety, conflict, or possible harm
surface_steps.reason: The premium is presence, care, and accountability. Students should not receive automated reassurance or crisis-adjacent guidance.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: A student may feel dismissed, receive inappropriate guidance, or be routed away from a responsible adult.

surface_steps.step: Decide whether to escalate to a parent, counselor, administrator, or safeguarding process
surface_steps.current_owner: Program director or designated safeguarding lead
surface_steps.reason: AI may flag patterns for review, but escalation changes obligations and must belong to a named human authority.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: Unaccountable escalation, missed duty of care, unfair labeling, or privacy breach.

surface_steps.step: Generate practice options or study next steps after human review
surface_steps.reason: AI can draft practice menus, reminders, and study plans once the human has checked the student's actual need and constraints.
surface_steps.risk_if_automated: Generic advice may blame the student, overload them, or ignore access/support barriers.

ai_roles: Collect routine logistics with a human-contact fallback
Draft private support summaries for teacher review
Suggest practice options after a human checks fit
Flag missing context for a named human owner instead of making escalation decisions
protected_human_work: Discipline, grading, eligibility, or placement decisions
Promises about outcomes, accommodations, timelines, or obligations
human_checkpoints: Every support summary is reviewed by a named teacher, coach, or support lead before use
Students always see a direct human-help path before and after any form
Any harm, safety, discrimination, disability, or family-status signal bypasses automation and goes to the designated human owner
Pilot review after 20 support requests or 30 days, whichever comes first
boundary_notes: Synthetic/example data only; no real student records or private education data.
Review aid only; not legal, medical, counseling, special-education, employment, or compliance advice.
AI can remove support-office drag; it does not replace the adult who carries duty of care.
